body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:restart

## Scenario

subagent-orphan-cleanup: a parent session launches a foreground subagent, then the parent process is abruptly killed / restarted WHILE the child turn is still in flight, leaving the child orphaned. The daemon's pid_manager sweep must reap both sessions cleanly — process_exited for parent and child — WITHOUT a spurious state_transition to working (or waiting) for the orphaned child during cleanup. Regression coverage for issue #321 (zombie-on-restart). User-observable signal (scenario-meanings): both rows disappear after the restart sweep, and the orphaned child never flips to working/waiting during cleanup.

## Agent supports it (agent_supports: yes)

opencode ships a first-class subagent primitive: the `task` tool spawns a child session whose `session` row carries `parent_id = <parent_session_id>`, and the parent's turn stays open across the child's lifetime (the `task` tool part stays `running` until the child reports back). This is exactly the parent+child shape the orphan scenario needs, and it is ALREADY recorded live as opencode/foreground-subagent — so a parent can be driven into the working span with a live child whose turn is mid-flight (e.g. a child running `bash sleep 30`). The orphaning is then created by killing the parent process group while that child turn is still in flight.

## daemon observes it (daemon_capability: full)

opencode is the lone `ProcessOwnedStore` adapter (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/agent.go`): the daemon reads session/turn state straight from the SQLite store and the parent_id graph. Child birth + linkage is proven observable by the foreground-subagent recording (the watcher reads `parent_id` per `session` row and emits `EventNewSession` with `ParentSessionID`, promoted to transcript_new + parent_linked). The orphan-cleanup invariant itself rides the pid_manager / restart sweep, which is transport-agnostic: when the parent process group dies, the daemon emits process_exited for the parent's and the orphaned child's sessions WITHOUT parsing any new store activity — there is no new turn part to read, so no state_transition to working/waiting fires for the orphaned child. readyTTL then evicts the rows. The #321 invariant is the same independently of the source transport (it is the sweep, not the parser, that must avoid the spurious working transition), so the daemon side is `full` — distinct from the opencode error-class cells (turn-aborted-by-error / token-quota-exhausted) where the GAP is a store part opencode never writes; here no store part is needed at all, the cleanup is process-exit-driven.

## Why this cell is NOT recordable today (driver gap: restart)

The scenario requires SIGKILLing / abruptly restarting the parent opencode process group WHILE a subagent turn is still in flight, so the child is left orphaned rather than reaped by its own clean exit. The opencode interactive driver (`.claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scripts/drive-opencode-interactive.sh`) drives opencode through BLOCKING headless `opencode run` subprocesses and implements ONLY three step types in its `case "$TYPE"` dispatch: `send` (launches an `opencode run` subprocess and WAITS for it to complete), `wait_turn` (a no-op — `opencode run` already blocks until the turn ends), and `sleep`. There is NO `sigkill` / `restart` (a.k.a. `kill`) step, and structurally there is no way to orphan a child mid-turn: each `send` BLOCKS inside the `opencode run` subprocess until the parent's turn — and therefore every concurrent `task` child — has already completed. By the time the driver regains control there is no in-flight subagent turn left to orphan, and headless `opencode run` exits cleanly at turn end (a graceful exit, NOT the abrupt mid-subagent kill the cell needs). Eliciting a genuine orphan requires holding the opencode process alive across the child's lifetime and killing its process group from the OUTSIDE while the child turn streams — a primitive this send-only blocking-subprocess driver cannot express.

## Not a false gap (and not caught by recipe-lint)

The claudecode row for this scenario uses a `sigkill` step in its recipe (it kills the entire claude process group, leaving the child orphaned). The opencode driver has no equivalent: it owns no long-lived process to signal — it shells out to `opencode run` per turn and that subprocess is already gone by the time a step could fire. Unlike a slash command that takes an inline argument (sendable via the `send` path), a mid-turn process-group kill is fundamentally an out-of-band process-control primitive against a running turn; there is no send-only equivalent. recipe-lint.sh does NOT flag this: the recipe is left `applicable:false` with no `script`, so there is no step-TYPE for the lint to reject — the gap is the absence of a live-REPL/process-control CAPABILITY, mirroring the opencode user-esc-interrupt (gap:interrupt) and model-switch-midsession (gap:model-select) driver_gaps, which recipe-lint likewise could not catch.

## What would unblock it

Replace or augment `drive-opencode-interactive.sh` with a LIVE-REPL / process-control driver (analogous to the tmux-based claudecode/pi/aider drivers): launch the opencode process so it stays alive across the child's lifetime, `send` a prompt that spawns a long-running `task` subagent (e.g. read README.md then `bash sleep 30`), wait for parent_linked, then fire a `sigkill`/`restart` step that kills the parent's process group mid-turn. Once the driver can orphan an in-flight child, the cell becomes recordable: the daemon already reaps orphaned sessions via the pid_manager sweep without a spurious working transition, so the #321 invariant in expected.jsonl would validate.

## Confidence

0.85: the driver grammar (`send|wait_turn|sleep`, blocking `opencode run` per send, no sigkill/restart step) is read directly from the live driver source, and the parent_id child-linkage observability is proven by the existing opencode/foreground-subagent recording. Not higher only because the unblocked recording itself (a real orphaned-child process_exited flowing through the cleanup sweep with no working transition) has not been captured on opencode's store transport — that is the developer task this gap defers.
caveats: Driver-gap, not an agent or daemon limitation: opencode the agent supports subagents (task tool, proven by opencode/foreground-subagent) and the daemon reaps orphaned sessions via the transport-agnostic pid_manager/restart sweep. The blocker is purely the driver's missing process-control primitive to SIGKILL/restart the parent process group mid-subagent (gap:restart, a.k.a. gap:sigkill).

recipe-lint.sh does NOT catch this gap: the recipe is left applicable:false with no script, so there is no step-TYPE to reject; the missing capability is an out-of-band mid-turn process-group kill against a blocking `opencode run` subprocess (same shape as the opencode user-esc-interrupt gap:interrupt / model-switch-midsession gap:model-select cells).

Headless `opencode run` exits CLEANLY at turn end — that graceful exit is NOT the orphaning primitive this cell needs; the child must be left parentless by an abrupt mid-flight kill of the parent process group, not by the parent finishing its turn.

daemon_capability:full rides the process-exit/restart sweep (no store part required for the orphaned child), distinct from the opencode turn-aborted-by-error / token-quota-exhausted cells where the gap is a store part opencode never writes.

sources.note: case "$TYPE" handles only send|wait_turn|sleep; each `send` launches a BLOCKING `opencode run` subprocess and waits for it to complete — no sigkill/restart step, and no long-lived process to signal mid-turn (the subprocess is already gone by the time control returns)

proves opencode spawns subagents via the `task` tool with parent_id linkage observed from the SQLite store (transcript_new + parent_linked) — agent_supports:yes for the parent+child shape this cell orphans
recorded child birth/linkage phases this orphan spec reuses up to parent_linked before the kill

opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter; daemon reads session/turn state + parent_id graph from SQLite. The orphan-cleanup sweep is process-exit-driven and transport-agnostic, so no new store part is needed to reap the orphaned child

reference spec for the orphan arc (parent_session_birth → parent_turn_start → child_session_birth → parent_linked → parent_killed → child_cleanup with the no-working/waiting invariant); generalized here to opencode's store transport with looser delays
claudecode subagent-orphan-cleanup recipe uses a `sigkill` step (kills the claude process group); opencode has no long-lived process to signal — it shells out to `opencode run` per turn
